TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_bala', title = bala', year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/bala'} }

S.FORM: == bala' == Ťāļ u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. siwkay nu balat u bala' sa, u kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw atu aadupan. anu mihanhan kita, macumuden ku bali tayza i bala', zikuzan sa, tayza tu labu nuizang. sisa mauzip kita. u kakalimaan a kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw. buting, kacumuli...izaw tu ku bala'. u pacucu'ay a aadupan sa , situsa a bala'. pabeli ku bali katukuh i kidu-mahmin. pabeli ku O2,mibalic ku CO2. kyu mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika "Ťāļś≥°". The lungs are the primary organs of the respiratory system in humans and many other animals including a few fish and some snails. In mammals and most other vertebrates, two lungs are located near the backbone on either side of the heart. Their function in the respiratory system is to extract oxygen from the atmosphere and transfer it into the bloodstream, and to release carbon dioxide from the bloodstream into the atmosphere, in a process of gas exchange. Respiration is driven by different muscular systems in different species. Mammals, reptiles and birds use their different muscles to support and foster breathing. In early tetrapods, air was driven into the lungs by the pharyngeal muscles via buccal pumping, a mechanism still seen in amphibians. In humans, the main muscle of respiration that drives breathing is the diaphragm. The lungs also provide airflow that makes vocal sounds including human speech possible.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺa Ē~ ° == * u aŇčaŇčan nu ńßanńßan ita ku bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. siwkaj nu bar~…ĺat u bar~…ĺa Ē~ ° sa, u kidu-sakar~…ĺuk nu tad…ômaw atu aadupan. anu mińßanńßan kita, ma ¶umud…ôn ku bar~…ĺi tajza i bar~…ĺa Ē~ °, zikuzan sa, tajza tu r~…ĺabu nuizaŇč. sisa mauzip kita. u kakar~…ĺimaan a kidu-sakar~…ĺuk nu tad…ômaw. butiŇč, ka ¶umur~…ĺi...izaw tu ku bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. u pa ¶u ¶u Ē~ °aj a aadupan sa , situsa a bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. pab…ôr~…ĺi ku bar~…ĺi katukuńß i kidu-mańßmin. pab…ôr~…ĺi ku o*,mibar~…ĺi ¶ ku ¶o*. kju mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u aŇčaŇčan nu ńßanńßan ita ku bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika "**". tńß…ô r~…ĺuŇčs ar…ô tńß…ô primarj organs of tńß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm in ńßumans and manj otńß…ôr animar~…ĺs in ¶r~…ĺudiŇč a f…ôw fisńß and som…ô snair~…ĺs. in mammar~…ĺs and most otńß…ôr *…ôrt…ôbrat…ôs, two r~…ĺuŇčs ar…ô r~…ĺo ¶at…ôd n…ôar tńß…ô ba ¶kbon…ô on …ôitńß…ôr sid…ô of tńß…ô ńß…ôart. tńß…ôir fun ¶tion in tńß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm is to …ô*tra ¶t o*jg…ôn from tńß…ô atmospńß…ôr…ô and transf…ôr it into tńß…ô br~…ĺoodstr…ôam, and to r…ôr~…ĺ…ôas…ô ¶arbon dio*id…ô from tńß…ô br~…ĺoodstr…ôam into tńß…ô atmospńß…ôr…ô, in a pro ¶…ôss of gas …ô* ¶ńßaŇč…ô. *…ôspiration is dri*…ôn bj diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶ur~…ĺar sjst…ôms in diff…ôr…ônt sp…ô ¶i…ôs. mammar~…ĺs, r…ôptir~…ĺ…ôs and birds us…ô tńß…ôir diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶r~…ĺ…ôs to support and fost…ôr br…ôatńßiŇč. in …ôarr~…ĺj t…ôtrapods, air was dri*…ôn into tńß…ô r~…ĺuŇčs bj tńß…ô pńßarjŇč…ôar~…ĺ mus ¶r~…ĺ…ôs *ia bu ¶ ¶ar~…ĺ pumpiŇč, a m…ô ¶ńßanism stir~…ĺr~…ĺ s…ô…ôn in ampńßibians. in ńßumans, tńß…ô main mus ¶r~…ĺ…ô of r…ôspiration tńßat dri*…ôs br…ôatńßiŇč is tńß…ô diapńßragm. tńß…ô r~…ĺuŇčs ar~…ĺso pro*id…ô airfr~…ĺow tńßat mak…ôs *o ¶ar~…ĺ sounds in ¶r~…ĺudiŇč ńßuman sp…ô…ô ¶ńß possibr~…ĺ…ô.
S.FORM: == bala' == Ťāļ u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. siwkay nu balat u bala' sa, u kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw atu aadupan. anu mihanhan kita, macumuden ku bali tayza i bala', zikuzan sa, tayza tu labu nuizang. sisa mauzip kita. u kakalimaan a kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw. buting, kacumuli...izaw tu ku bala'. u pacucu'ay a aadupan sa , situsa a bala'. pabeli ku bali katukuh i kidu-mahmin. pabeli ku O2,mibalic ku CO2. kyu mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika "Ťāļś≥°". The lungs are the primary organs of the respiratory system in humans and many other animals including a few fish and some snails. In mammals and most other vertebrates, two lungs are located near the backbone on either side of the heart. Their function in the respiratory system is to extract oxygen from the atmosphere and transfer it into the bloodstream, and to release carbon dioxide from the bloodstream into the atmosphere, in a process of gas exchange. Respiration is driven by different muscular systems in different species. Mammals, reptiles and birds use their different muscles to support and foster breathing. In early tetrapods, air was driven into the lungs by the pharyngeal muscles via buccal pumping, a mechanism still seen in amphibians. In humans, the main muscle of respiration that drives breathing is the diaphragm. The lungs also provide airflow that makes vocal sounds including human speech possible.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺa Ē~ ° == * u aŇčaŇčan nu ńßanńßan ita ku bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. siwkaj nu bar~…ĺat u bar~…ĺa Ē~ ° sa, u kidu-sakar~…ĺuk nu tad…ômaw atu aadupan. anu mińßanńßan kita, ma ¶umud…ôn ku bar~…ĺi tajza i bar~…ĺa Ē~ °, zikuzan sa, tajza tu r~…ĺabu nuizaŇč. sisa mauzip kita. u kakar~…ĺimaan a kidu-sakar~…ĺuk nu tad…ômaw. butiŇč, ka ¶umur~…ĺi...izaw tu ku bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. u pa ¶u ¶u Ē~ °aj a aadupan sa , situsa a bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. pab…ôr~…ĺi ku bar~…ĺi katukuńß i kidu-mańßmin. pab…ôr~…ĺi ku o*,mibar~…ĺi ¶ ku ¶o*. kju mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u aŇčaŇčan nu ńßanńßan ita ku bar~…ĺa Ē~ °. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika "**". tńß…ô r~…ĺuŇčs ar…ô tńß…ô primarj organs of tńß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm in ńßumans and manj otńß…ôr animar~…ĺs in ¶r~…ĺudiŇč a f…ôw fisńß and som…ô snair~…ĺs. in mammar~…ĺs and most otńß…ôr *…ôrt…ôbrat…ôs, two r~…ĺuŇčs ar…ô r~…ĺo ¶at…ôd n…ôar tńß…ô ba ¶kbon…ô on …ôitńß…ôr sid…ô of tńß…ô ńß…ôart. tńß…ôir fun ¶tion in tńß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm is to …ô*tra ¶t o*jg…ôn from tńß…ô atmospńß…ôr…ô and transf…ôr it into tńß…ô br~…ĺoodstr…ôam, and to r…ôr~…ĺ…ôas…ô ¶arbon dio*id…ô from tńß…ô br~…ĺoodstr…ôam into tńß…ô atmospńß…ôr…ô, in a pro ¶…ôss of gas …ô* ¶ńßaŇč…ô. *…ôspiration is dri*…ôn bj diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶ur~…ĺar sjst…ôms in diff…ôr…ônt sp…ô ¶i…ôs. mammar~…ĺs, r…ôptir~…ĺ…ôs and birds us…ô tńß…ôir diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶r~…ĺ…ôs to support and fost…ôr br…ôatńßiŇč. in …ôarr~…ĺj t…ôtrapods, air was dri*…ôn into tńß…ô r~…ĺuŇčs bj tńß…ô pńßarjŇč…ôar~…ĺ mus ¶r~…ĺ…ôs *ia bu ¶ ¶ar~…ĺ pumpiŇč, a m…ô ¶ńßanism stir~…ĺr~…ĺ s…ô…ôn in ampńßibians. in ńßumans, tńß…ô main mus ¶r~…ĺ…ô of r…ôspiration tńßat dri*…ôs br…ôatńßiŇč is tńß…ô diapńßragm. tńß…ô r~…ĺuŇčs ar~…ĺso pro*id…ô airfr~…ĺow tńßat mak…ôs *o ¶ar~…ĺ sounds in ¶r~…ĺudiŇč ńßuman sp…ô…ô ¶ńß possibr~…ĺ…ô.
